sourceMovie.title: The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear (1991) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David Zucker's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear clicked for you, The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad! (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear?
faq.answer: Try The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad!, Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery, Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me—each shares DNA with The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear?
faq.answer: The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear (1991) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Naked Gun Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
